input.background: Refer to the advertising-monthly-sales data presented in Table 11.1. Consider the straight-line model, $E(y)=\beta_0+\beta_1 x$, where $y=$ sales revenue (thousands of dollars) and $x=$ advertising expenditure (hundreds of dollars).
input.question: Use the method of least squares to estimate the values of $\beta_0$ and $\beta_1$.

output.answer:
We used Excel to make the preliminary computations for finding the least squares line. The Excel spreadsheet is shown in Figure 11.5. Using the values on the spreadsheet, we find

$$
\begin{aligned}
\bar{x} & =\frac{\sum x}{5}=\frac{15}{5}=3 \
\bar{y} & =\frac{\sum y}{5}=\frac{10}{5}=2 \
\mathrm{SS}_{x y} & =\sum(x-\bar{x})(y-\bar{y})=\sum(x-3)(y-2)=7 \
\mathrm{SS}_{x x} & =\sum(x-\bar{x})^2=\sum(x-3)^2=10
\end{aligned}
$$

Then the slope of the least squares line is

$$
\hat{\beta}_1=\frac{\mathrm{SS}_{x y}}{\mathrm{SS}_{x x}}=\frac{7}{10}=.7
$$
and the $y$-intercept is

$$
\hat{\beta}_0=\bar{y}-\hat{\beta}_1 \bar{x}=2-(.7)(3)=2-2.1=-.1
$$

The least squares line is thus

$$
\hat{y}=\hat{\beta}_0+\hat{\beta}_1 x=-.1+.7 x
$$

The graph of this line is shown in Figure 11.6.
